name: Isleta del Moro - La Rellana Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular por el Sendero Requena
description: This difficult 6.1-mile (9.8 km) trail running loop in Cabo de Gata offers challenging volcanic terrain and stunning coastal views.
region_intro: Jogging around National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar offers routes through a unique volcanic landscape, characterized by dramatic rock formations, sharp peaks, and coastal cliffs. The region features over 30 miles (50 km) of wild beaches and coves, providing extensive coastal running opportunities. Runners can also explore the flat terrain of the Salt Flats (Las Salinas de Cabo de Gata) and the distinctive semi-desert hinterland. This diverse terrain provides a variety of running experiences across the park. Best jogging routes around National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar The most popular jogging route is Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular to Morrón de los Genoveses from San José, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the volcanic coastline. Another top favourite among local runners is Salinas de Cabo de Gata – running circular from la Torre de San Miguel, a moderate 8.1 miles (13.1 km) path. This route provides flat terrain through the salt flats, known for their unique wetland ecosystem. Local runners also love the Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular by the Requena Trail, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through varied volcanic terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Jogging around National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar is defined by volcanic terrain, coastal trails, and salt flats. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1700 runners have used komoot to explore National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the running routes in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: The park offers a diverse range of terrain for runners. You'll encounter unique volcanic landscapes with dramatic rock formations and sharp peaks, extensive coastal trails along over 50 km of wild beaches and coves, and the flat, unique wetland ecosystem of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1060337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo de Gata Salt Pans&lt;/a&gt;. There's also a semi-desert hinterland, providing a distinctive arid backdrop for your runs.

Q: How many running routes are available in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: There are over 120 running routes available in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. These include a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the park?
A: Yes, the park offers several easier routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986453743/salinas-de-cabo-de-gata-running-circular-from-la-torre-de-san-miguel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Cabo de Gata – running circular from la Torre de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.1-mile (13.1 km) path that provides flat terrain through the salt flats, making it suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I see while jogging in the park?
A: Jogging in Cabo de Gata-Níjar allows you to experience stunning natural beauty and historical sites. You can see dramatic volcanic cliffs, pristine beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/125186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Genoveses Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/125188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mónsul Beach&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1060337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo de Gata Salt Pans&lt;/a&gt;, home to migratory birds. Historical elements like abandoned mines, old fortifications, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1060340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel Tower&lt;/a&gt; also dot the landscape.

Q: Are there any running routes with great viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer spectacular panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985282811/parque-natural-de-cabo-de-gata-trail-running-circular-to-morron-de-los-genoveses-from-san-jose" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular to Morrón de los Genoveses from San José&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides views of the volcanic coastline. You can also find strategic viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granatilla viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive vistas of the protected area.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: The running routes in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Runners frequently praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the breathtaking coastal views, and the variety of terrain that makes each run a distinct experience.

Q: Are there challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Yes, the park features several difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985274488/parque-natural-de-cabo-de-gata-trail-running-circular-by-the-requena-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular by the Requena Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.1-mile (9.8 km) route through varied volcanic terrain with substantial climbs. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985198069/natural-park-of-cabo-de-gata-trail-running-through-barranco-hondo-and-mesa-roldan-sl-a-166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Cabo de Gata – trail running through Barranco Hondo and Mesa Roldán&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 13.5 km with considerable elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: Given its hot desert climate, the best time to jog in Cabo de Gata-Níjar is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late evening runs more advisable if visiting during peak season.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Many of the routes in the park are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985270855/parque-natural-de-cabo-de-gata-trail-running-circular-at-cerro-de-la-hortichuela-from-las-negras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular at Cerro de la Hortichuela from Las Negras&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.5-mile (13.7 km) loop.

Q: Is it possible to reach the running trails using public transport?
A: While some villages like San José, the park's main hub, are accessible by public transport, direct access to all trailheads via public transport can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider car rental for greater flexibility in reaching specific starting points for your runs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths in the National Park?
A: For families, routes with flatter terrain and shorter distances are generally more suitable. The paths around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1060337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo de Gata Salt Pans&lt;/a&gt;, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986453743/salinas-de-cabo-de-gata-running-circular-from-la-torre-de-san-miguel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Cabo de Gata – running circular from la Torre de San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, offer a relatively flat and engaging experience, perfect for a family outing with unique birdwatching opportunities.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the jogging routes in National Park Cabo de Gata-Níjar?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within the National Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. It's always a good idea to check specific local regulations or signage at trailheads, especially concerning protected areas or beaches during certain seasons.
tips: The restaurants in the village are famous for the "pescaito", freshly caught by the colourful boats in the area. The beauty of the place, in the Cabo de gata-Nijar Natural Park, is undeniable.
A cove of black sand about 300 m long that is reached by a path lined with pine trees and palm trees.

This trail takes us into the Natural Park to offer us almost aerial views of the coast, from almost 500 meters above sea level, and allows us to see the Barrancos del Negro and Requena, with a density of vegetation that will surprise us, thanks to the abundance of heart of palm, the only native European palm tree.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular by the Requena Trail?
faqs.answer: This route is considered a "Hard run" for trail running. It demands good physical fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy, cushioned shoes due to narrow paths and loose rocks. While rated "Medium" for hiking, its significant elevation changes and rugged terrain make it challenging for jogging.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses volcanic sierras with steep slopes and rugged terrain, including ravines. You'll find a mix of surfaces: approximately 4.32 km unpaved, 2.44 km gravel, 1.81 km natural paths, and some shorter paved sections. Be prepared for loose rocks in certain areas.

faqs.question: What are the main sights or highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking "almost aerial views of the coast" and expansive panoramic vistas of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park. Key highlights include the View of Isleta del Moro, the Cove of the Bulls, and the distinctive Requena Trail itself, known for its volcanic landscapes and dense palmito palms in the Barranco de Requena.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a "Hard run" for trail running, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. The challenging terrain and significant elevation changes make it more suitable for experienced trail runners.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, trails in the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park typically have designated parking areas near popular access points. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's starting point for the most accurate parking information before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata – trail running circular by the Requena Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park generally allows dogs, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Given the rugged terrain and potential for loose rocks, ensure your dog is fit for such a challenging route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to run this trail is typically during the cooler months, from autumn to spring (October to May). Summers in Cabo de Gata can be extremely hot, making trail running challenging and potentially unsafe due to heat exposure and lack of shade.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park or this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required to access the trails within the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, including the Requena Trail. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife or vegetation.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for trail running on this route?
faqs.answer: You should wear sturdy, cushioned trail running shoes with good grip due to the narrow paths and loose rocks. Bring plenty of water, as the environment is arid and there are no water sources on the trail. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is essential, and consider bringing a light jacket for higher elevations or cooler mornings.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other official trails. Approximately 26% of the route follows the Senda Azul de Cabo de Gata, 16% is part of the Transandalus, and 32% is on the Sendero Requena itself.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The environment is largely arid, but you can spot characteristic Mediterranean flora such as palmito (the only native European palm tree, notably abundant in the Requena ravine), esparto, albaida, and cornical. There are also remnants of a small relict pine forest. Wildlife is typically shy, but you might see various bird species, reptiles, and small mammals adapted to the arid landscape.
